label: noncommutation
classification_reason: The witness does not invalidate the MSM-CVaR endpoint or the pointwise-band comparator. It pins the value of the conjectured strict-gap clause in the proposed two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell strict-overlap MSM class: the common upper-tail rearrangement vertex optimizes every generated loss, so \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)=\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) and \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=0\), not \(>0\). The confirmed nested-endpoint sibling anchors the retained endpoint structure, and the counterexample itself supplies the corrected finite-class value.
witness_property: In the degenerate-X finite-outcome MSM response-type LP, each action cell has bounded density-ratio variables \(q_{aj}=p_{aj}r_{aj}\) with \(\ell_a\le r_{aj}\le u_a\) and \(\sum_j p_{aj}r_{aj}=1\). Greedy upper-tail rearrangement gives a single vertex \(q_a^\star\) that simultaneously minimizes every lower CDF threshold and maximizes every increasing generated loss \((Y-\eta)_+\); the policy mixture of these vertices is therefore a first-order-stochastically maximal member of the pointwise band.

mend.description: Retain the original objects and hypotheses, retain the global nonnegativity comparison, and replace the strict-existence clause for the proposed finite MSM class by the witness-implied equality \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=0\) throughout that class. Strict positive gaps outside common-tilt regimes are not asserted by the repaired result.
mend.justification_cite: DornGuo2021QuantileBalancing, arXiv:2102.04543 / DOI:10.48550/arXiv.2102.04543, for sharp marginal-sensitivity partial identification through bounded propensity-weight LPs and monotone quantile-balancing rearrangement.

vacuity.witness_member: Take \(X=x_0\) a.s., \(A\in\{0,1\}\) with \(P(A=0\mid X)=P(A=1\mid X)=1/2\), \(\Gamma=2\), \(\pi=(1/2,1/2)\), and \(P(Y=j\mid A=a,X=x_0)=1/3\) for \(j=0,1,2\) and both actions. This is the explicit strict-overlap finite LP member used in the witness, with \(q_a^\star=(1/4,1/4,1/2)\).

substance.motivating_regime_retained: The repaired claim keeps the motivating fixed-policy robust MSM-CVaR regime with strict overlap, \(1<\Gamma<\infty\), the pointwise-CDF band comparator, and the proposed two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell class; it changes only the claimed value of the noncommutation gap in that class.

naturalness.reason: No witness-tailored hypothesis is added. The correction uses the standard sharp marginal-sensitivity bounded-weight LP and monotone rearrangement/quantile-balancing convention used before seeing this witness.

lineage_note: The original claim that the two-action, three-outcome, one-cell class has \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}>0\) was refuted by the common upper-tail MSM vertex \(q^\star\); it is mended to global \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}\ge0\) plus finite-class \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}=0\) via a rate/value correction with no added hypothesis; the rearrangement convention is justified by DornGuo2021QuantileBalancing, arXiv:2102.04543.
mended_headline_statement: Confirmed rate-corrected noncommutation result. Under Assumptions~\ref{ass:iid-overlap}--\ref{ass:tail}, the pointwise-band comparator is conservative relative to the sharp nested MSM-CVaR endpoint: \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)\ge \underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\). In the proposed two-action, three-outcome, one-covariate-cell strict-overlap MSM response-type class with \(1<\Gamma<\infty\), the common monotone upper-tail tilt is simultaneously optimal for every generated loss \((Y-\eta)_+\), hence \(W_{\tau,\Gamma}^{\mathrm{band}}(\pi;P)=\underline W_{\tau,\Gamma}(\pi;P)\) and \(\Delta_{\mathrm{nc}}(\pi;P)=0\) throughout that class.
